SpTU 2, 004 [unidentified incantation or ritual][via cams/gkab]

Obverse

Column ii
o¬†iio¬†ii¬† (traces of 3 lines)
o¬†ii¬†4o¬†ii¬†4

[...] e‚āā-a Šłęe‚āā-ni-ib‚āā-SAR?-x

[...] may he ... in the house (Akkadian has: [...] ... pure ... half).

[...] x TU NU KU‚āÉti ma?-aŇ°‚āā?-lu? x x x x
o¬†ii¬†5o¬†ii¬†5

[...] x x x ‚łĘBI?‚ł£ ur-saŇč an-na-ke‚āĄ

[...] ... the hero of An (Akkadian has: [...] ... at the second time the hero of Anu)

[...] x KUR ina 2e qar-rad da-nu1
o¬†ii¬†6o¬†ii¬†6

[...] x ‚łĘe‚āā?‚ł£-a Šłęe‚āā-ni-ib‚āā-Šłęu-luŠłę-Šłęa

[...] ... may he frighten him in the house.

x x x x x li-gal-lit-su
o¬†ii¬†7o¬†ii¬†7

[...] x x x ‚łĘe‚āā?‚ł£-a Šłęe‚āā-ni-ib‚āā-zi-zi

[...] ... may he tear him out in the house.

[...] NU? ina x x x x RI? ina E‚āā li-is-suŠłę-Ň°u‚āā
o¬†ii¬†8o¬†ii¬†8

[...] TUM‚āÉ x x x KID? e‚āā-a Šłęe‚āā-ni-ib‚āā-zi-zi

[...] ... may he tear him out in the house.

[...] x x KAB? x x x ina E‚āā li-is-suŠłę-Ň°u‚āā
o¬†ii¬†9o¬†ii¬†9

[...] x x KID? esir me-te diŇčir-re-e-ne-ke‚āĄ e‚āā-a Šłęe‚āā-ni-ib‚āā-zi-zi

[...] ... with bitumen befitting the gods may he tear him out in the house.

[...] x TI ina ESIR si-mat? DINGIR-MEŇ† ina E‚āā li-‚łĘis-suŠłę‚ł£-Ň°u‚āā
(traces of 1 line)
(rest of column missing)
Reverse
Column i
(start of column missing)
r¬†i¬†1'r¬†1'

[...] x [...]

[...] ... [...] (Akkadian has: [...] behind you ... [...] ...)

[x x] ‚łĘEGIR‚ł£-ka x x [x] A RU?
r¬†i¬†2'r¬†2'

[x] x e‚āā-a-ta a-ra-an;Šłęur-Šłęur-re

From the [...] ... of the house, I have drawn you (Akkadian has: [...] curse, in the house I drew you).

[x x] ma-mi-tu‚āĄ ina E‚āā e-ŠĻ£ir-ka
r¬†i¬†3'r¬†3'

‚łĘzag‚ł£-du‚āą e‚āā-a-ta esir im-babbar a-ra-an;tag-tag-ga

From the door posts of the house, I have smeared you with bitumen (and) gypsum.

sip-pi E‚āā iŠĻ≠-ŠĻ≠a‚āā gaŠĻ£-ŠĻ£a u‚āā-lap-pit-ka
r¬†i¬†4'r¬†4'

zi diŇčir gal-gal-e-ne-ke‚āĄ i-ri-pad‚āÉ ¬†; Šłęa-ba-ra-du-un

I have adjured you by the life of the great gods, you should depart.

ni-iŇ° DINGIR-MEŇ† GAL-MEŇ† u‚āā-tam-mi-ka ¬†; lu-u‚āā ta-at-tal-lak
(2 columns missing)

1On the tablet the Akkadian equivalents to lines 5 and 6 both follow line 6.
